title: Tanacetum podlechii
intro: Tanacetum podelechii, also known as Tanacetum, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum podelechii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tanacetum podelechii include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Tanacetum podlechii is classified as a species within the genus Tanacetum and the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the order Asterales and the clade Eudicots. The class of this plant is Magnoliopsida, which is a part of the larger group of flowering plants called angiosperms. Tanacetum podlechii further belongs to the subclass Rosidae, and then the subclass is divided into the superorder Asteranae. Tanacetum podlechii is also a part of the infraclass Asteridae.
